summary.intent: Fix ARIA accessibility issues in the accordion component based on Codex code review feedback on PR #662, specifically removing invalid aria-multiselectable from the root element and adding aria-disabled to non-collapsible open triggers.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented both ARIA fixes with regression tests, updated snapshots and spec documentation, passed all verification gates (unit tests, proptests, spec validation, coverage, mutation testing), and committed the review fix to PR #662.

repo: The repo uses a 'waiting-for-codex-review' workflow where @codex is triggered via PR comment after first push, and the agent polls for 👀/👍 reactions plus review threads.
PRs that create .snap files require a 'snapshot-reviewed' label to be applied.
The verification pipeline includes: unit tests, spec conformance tests, proptests (--ignored), insta snapshot review, cargo xtask spec validate, llvm-cov coverage, and cargo-mutants mutation testing.
The spec lives in spec/components/navigation/accordion.md (and leptos/dioxus variants) and must be kept in sync with implementation changes.
aria-multiselectable is invalid on a role-less accordion root element.
Non-collapsible open triggers should use aria-disabled (not native disabled) to remain focusable.

code.finding: Accordion root was incorrectly emitting aria-multiselectable; this attribute is invalid for elements without an explicit role that supports selection containers.
Non-collapsible open accordion triggers needed aria-disabled=true added without native disabled, so they remain keyboard-focusable.
Snapshot for multiple-root accordion lost aria-multiselectable attribute after the fix.
Snapshot for open non-collapsible trigger now shows aria-disabled=true.
Spec still described old aria-multiselectable root behavior and needed updating to remove that and document aria-disabled for non-collapsible open triggers.

workflow: INSTA_UPDATE=always cargo test is the pattern for regenerating insta snapshots in bulk.
cargo mutants -p <crate> -f <file> scopes mutation testing to a specific file, which is important for keeping run times manageable.
cargo-mutants can run silently for extended periods (multiple minutes) without streaming progress; checking ps is needed to confirm the process is still alive.
The regression-test-first approach (write failing tests, then implement) is the expected workflow for review fixes in this repo.
The full verification sequence before committing a review fix is: unit tests → spec conformance → proptests → insta → xtask spec validate → llvm-cov → cargo-mutants → clippy.

friction: cargo-mutants ran for an unusually long time (many minutes) with no output, requiring multiple process-alive checks via ps to confirm it hadn't silently failed.
Codex review took significantly longer than usual (multiple 2-minute polling cycles with 👀 but no threads) before finally posting feedback.
The local commit hook caused a longer-than-normal delay on the initial commit, requiring an explicit wait.
open_items: The leptos and dioxus component spec files (spec/leptos-components/navigation/accordion.md, spec/dioxus-components/navigation/accordion.md) were checked but may not have been updated if they also referenced the old aria-multiselectable behavior.
Codex review loop needs to be restarted after the fix commit is pushed — the transcript ends at commit without confirming the @codex re-trigger comment was posted and the review completed.
